sections.paragraph:
εἰρήνη, ης, ἡ [etym. uncertain] central mng. ‘peace’

—1. ‘state of harmony’, peace

—a. as result from cessation of hostility: political entities Lk 14:32; Ac 12:20; individuals 7:26.

—b. of peaceful condition with focus on personal relationships relating esp. to acceptance of one another in common objectives Mt 10:34; Ro 3:17; Gal 5:22; Eph 4:3; Hb 12:14.

—2. ‘state of well-being’, peace

—a. Hebraically as greeting(s) Mt 10:13; Mk 5:34; Lk 24:36; 2 J 3; Rv 1:4.

—b. as characteristic of the Messianic age and divine favor Lk 1:79; 2:14; J 16:33; Ac 10:36; Ro 2:10; Col 3:15.
